onboarding.question: Define whether the Auth Sheet should use returning-user framing ("Welcome back.") or neutral framing ("Sign in or sign up.") when the same sheet serves both new and returning users, since the messaging will influence how users perceive the onboarding moment.
onboarding.designContext:
## Approved Product Spec
# Onboarding — Product Spec

## Problem
New and returning users need a frictionless path from first landing to the main chat home screen. Unauthenticated users risk losing their conversation history without realizing it, which undermines trust and conversion. The onboarding experience must get users to the home screen quickly, make the value of signing in clear without forcing it, and ensure no one is surprised by data loss.

## Target Users
- **New users** — arriving for the first time, no existing account
- **Returning users** — have an existing account, signing back in
- **Logged-out new users** — new users who choose to explore before signing up

## User Stories
1. As a new user, I want to sign up quickly so that I can start using Health360 without unnecessary friction.
2. As a returning user, I want to sign in and land directly on the home screen so that I can pick up where I left off.
3. As a logged-out user, I want to explore the product before committing to sign up so that I can understand its value first.
4. As a logged-out user, I want a subtle, persistent indicator that I am not signed in so that I am never surprised by the implications of being logged out.
5. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned before my session expires so that I can sign up and save my conversation before it's lost.
6. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned when I try to navigate away or close the tab so that I don't accidentally lose my conversation.
7. As a logged-out user, I want to know that signing in will preserve my conversation so that I have a clear reason to create an account at that moment.
8. As a new user completing sign-up from a logged-out session, I want my existing conversation to carry over so that I don't lose the context I've already built.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Unauthenticated users can access the chat interface and interact with the AI without signing in.
2. A persistent, non-dismissible indicator is visible to logged-out users displaying the text "Not signed in".
3. The indicator is read-only, displays no interactive elements, does not overlay other UI, and does not trigger any prompts or modals on its own.
4. The logged-out indicator remains visible at all times while the user is not authenticated, and disappears immediately upon valid authentication token receipt, before conversation history loads.
5. New users can sign up via SSO (Google, Apple Sign-In) only — no password-based registration.
6. Returning users can sign in via SSO and are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
7. New users who complete sign-up are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
8. When a logged-out user's session is approaching expiry with 10 minutes of inactivity time remaining AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a non-dismissible warning banner appears at the top of the chat interface with a "Sign in now" call-to-action button; the warning is automatically cleared only when the user performs an action that resets the inactivity timer (e.g., sends a message); the banner cannot be manually dismissed or swiped away.
9. When a logged-out user attempts to navigate away or close the tab AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a modal appears with a primary "Sign in" button and a secondary "Leave anyway" button; the modal has no dismiss affordance and the user must choose one of the two buttons to proceed; clicking "Leave anyway" permits the user to proceed with navigation or tab closure; browser-level force closes cannot be intercepted.
10. After the first complete AI response in a session, an in-conversation nudge surfaces during logged-out sessions only, informing the user that their conversation will not be saved unless they sign in, rendered as a conversational system message with a clear sign-up or sign-in call-to-action; the nudge is not displayed to authenticated users.
11. The in-conversation nudge fires exactly once per logged-out session; a new logged-out session begins each time the user is in an unauthenticated state, and if the user signs in and then signs out again within the same browser window, that constitutes a new logged-out session and the nudge fires again.
12. Both warnings and the in-conversation nudge include a clear call-to-action to sign up or sign in.
13. Upon completing sign-up or sign-in from any warning CTA, the user is taken directly to the main chat home screen and their previous logged-out conversation is immediately visible in the message thread without requiring additional navigation or explicit retrieval; system warning messages and the in-conversation nudge are excluded from the carried-over conversation thread; if conversation carry-over fails, an inline message appears confirming successful sign-in and explaining that the previous conversation could not be recovered.
14. If the logged-out user has no conversation history, no session expiry or navigation warning is shown.
15. When a logged-out user's session expires after inactivity, the chat interface remains interactive but becomes unresponsive — attempts to send a message trigger an inline error message at the top of the chat with a "Sign in" call-to-action; the user can dismiss the error and sign in at any time via the Auth Sheet.
16. When the AI is unavailable during a logged-out exploration session, an inline error message appears at the top of the chat interface; the user can send any new message to trigger a retry attempt; if the AI remains unavailable after a retry, the same inline error reappears and the user can attempt again with another message; the chat interface remains interactive.
17. There is no forced sign-up wall at any point in the logged-out experience.
18. The path from landing on the logged-out chat interface to authenticated home screen requires a maximum of 3 user interactions: (1) landing on the logged-out chat interface, (2) tapping a sign-in or sign-up call-to-action, (3) completing SSO and being taken directly to the authenticated home screen. No intermediate profile setup, goal-setting, or confirmation screens are permitted.
19. Goal setting does not occur during onboarding — it happens post-onboarding through the AI conversation.
20. On initial page load, the UI defaults to logged-out mode immediately while authentication state resolution occurs in the background.
21. If authentication state resolution succeeds during initial load, the logged-out indicator disappears immediately upon valid token receipt and conversation history becomes visible after valid token receipt; any conversation the user started during the auth resolution window is treated as part of the logged-out session and is carried over to the authenticated home screen's message thread; user drafts in the prompt bar are preserved across the auth state transition.
22. If authentication state resolution fails at initial load, the chat remains fully interactive in logged-out mode and a dismissible retry affordance appears inline at the top of the chat interface; the retry affordance persists until the user dismisses it, attempts retry, or navigates away from the page; the user can attempt retry at any point by tapping the affordance again; there is no limit on the number of retry attempts.
23. Unauthenticated sessions expire after 60 minutes of inactivity; each user action (message send, keystroke, tap) resets the inactivity timer.
24. Users land directly on the logged-out chat interface on first visit — there is no separate landing, splash, or onboarding screen; the chat interface is the primary entry point.
25. Tapping any "Sign in" or "Sign in now" call-to-action opens an Auth Sheet overlay displaying SSO provider buttons (Google, Apple Sign-In); the Auth Sheet is dismissible and returns the user to the logged-out chat.
26. Returning users and new users encounter the same Auth Sheet UI with identical SSO provider buttons and messaging; the SSO provider handles distinguishing between account creation (new user) and sign-in (returning user) transparently.
27. If a user's authentication token expires mid-session, the "Not signed in" indicator reappears automatically and all chat functionality becomes read-only — the user can view conversation history but cannot send new messages; attempting to send a message triggers an inline error; the user can sign in again via the Auth Sheet without losing conversation history.
28. Logged-out users receive generic, non-personalized conversational responses from the AI with no goals, no profile context, and no prior session memory; personalized responses and memory-based coaching are authenticated-only features that unlock upon sign-in.

## Edge Cases

- User signs up from the warning CTA but the session expires before authentication completes — conversation cannot be recovered; the user lands on the authenticated home screen with an inline message confirming successful sign-in and explaining that the previous conversation could not be recovered.
- User begins sign-up flow from the warning but abandons it — session continues to expire; if the user returns to the chat before expiry, the warning banner reappears if still within the expiry window.
- Conversation carry-over fails after successful authentication — user lands on the authenticated home screen with an inline message at the top confirming successful sign-in and explaining that the previous conversation could not be recovered; no retry mechanism is offered.
- User's logged-out session expires while they are away from the chat — the next time they attempt to send a message, they receive an inline error and can dismiss it and sign in at any time.
- User's authentication token expires mid-session while they are actively viewing the conversation — the "Not signed in" indicator reappears, chat becomes read-only, and the next message send attempt triggers an inline error; the user can sign in again via Auth Sheet and resume without data loss.

## Non-Goals
- Goal setting, profile building, and AI coaching — these begin after the user reaches the home screen.
- Password-based authentication — SSO only.
- Forced sign-up walls or conversion prompts outside of the defined warning triggers and in-conversation nudge.
- Auto-saving or server-side backup of logged-out conversations without explicit user action.
- Changing session expiry duration — infrastructure concern outside this spec.
- Social or referral flows — not part of the initial vision.

## Open Questions
(none)

## Current Design Draft
# Onboarding — Design Spec

## Figma

No Figma file at this stage — spec is the source of truth.

## Design Direction

**Dark mode primary — Archon Labs aesthetic (getarchon.dev).** Visual language: minimal, high negative space. The interface recedes; the conversation leads. Chrome is nearly invisible until needed.

**Archon Labs Visual Language:**
- Dark navy/charcoal background (#0A0A0F) with off-white text (#F8F8F7) — high contrast, clean
- Accent colors (violet #7C6FCD, teal #4FAFA8) used intentionally on interactive elements, labels, and highlights — never decorative
- Gradient text (135° violet → teal) on primary headings and UI chrome only
- Typography hierarchy: system-ui, weights 400/500/600, no custom fonts
- Signature treatment: dual independent pulsing glow effect. Violet and teal glows pulse independently (55–100% opacity cycle, 4s duration, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1), offset -1.8s on teal, 80px blur radius, maintained throughout session). Each glow instance is independent; home and Auth Sheet glows do not synchronize.
- Minimal chrome — buttons, cards, and surfaces are understated until interaction
- Extreme negative space — 60–70% empty on hero screens, 30–40% empty within containers

### Screen 1: Chat Home — Message Bubble Styling

**User Message Bubbles (Outgoing):**
- Background: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.12) (teal accent surface, 12% opacity)
- Text color: #F8F8F7 (primary text)
- Alignment: right-aligned, 12px margin from right edge
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px horizontal, 10px vertical
- Font: system-ui 14px 400 weight
- Max-width: 85% of conversation area width

**AI Response Bubbles (Incoming):**
- Background: transparent (no bubble background)
- Text color: #F8F8F7 (primary text)
- Alignment: left-aligned, 12px margin from left edge
- Border-radius: 0 (no rounding on text-only response)
- Padding: 12px horizontal, 10px vertical
- Font: system-ui 14px 400 weight
- Max-width: 100% of conversation area width (full width, no constraint)
- Accent styling: inline code blocks rendered with background rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.10) (violet accent surface), border-radius 6px, padding 4px 8px, monospace font

### Screen 1: Chat Home — Conversation-Active State

**Visual Treatment:**
- Conversation thread displays below top nav bar with full height scrollable container
- User messages render right-aligned with teal accent background (see User Message Bubbles above)
- AI responses render left-aligned with no background (see AI Response Bubbles above)
- Vertical spacing between messages: 16px gap
- Starter chips fade out on first AI message: 150ms parallel fade-out, ease-out easing
- Prompt bar remains pinned to bottom, always accessible
- Loading indicator (if AI is responding): spinner (12px diameter) + "Thinking..." text appears below last user message

## Brand

**Color Palette**
- `--bg`: #0A0A0F
- `--surface`: #13131A
- `--text`: #F8F8F7
- `--muted`: rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45)
- `--subtle`: rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.18)
- `--border`: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07)
- `--violet`: #7C6FCD
- `--teal`: #4FAFA8
- `--error`: #E06C75
- `--warning`: #E5C07B

**Glow (Signature Effect)**

Two independent radial glows — violet and teal — rendered separately and animated asynchronously.

**Violet glow:**
```css
background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.42) 0%, rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.14) 50%, transparent 100%);
filter: blur(80px);

@keyframes heartbeat-violet {
  0%   { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.97); }
  12%  { opacity: 1.00; transform: scale(1.20); }
  36%  { opacity: 0.90; transform: scale(1.13); }
  60%  { opacity: 0.58; transform: scale(0.98); }
  100% { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.97); }
}
animation: heartbeat-violet 4s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
```

**Teal glow:**
```css
background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.34) 0%, rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.11) 50%, transparent 100%);
filter: blur(80px);

@keyframes heartbeat-teal {
  0%   { opacity: 0.50; transform: scale(0.97); }
  12%  { opacity: 0.95; transform: scale(1.20); }
  36%  { opacity: 0.85; transform: scale(1.13); }
  60%  { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.98); }
  100% { opacity: 0.50; transform: scale(0.97); }
}
animation: heartbeat-teal 4s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
animation-delay: -1.8s;
```

**Usage rules:**
- Applied in exactly two places: (1) chat home background behind prompt bar, (2) Auth Sheet behind SSO buttons
- Positioned as a faint radial halo behind rounded pill containers and interactive elements
- Nowhere else in the product
- Each instance is independent — home glow and Auth Sheet glow never synchronize
- Both glows are decorative only (aria-hidden="true") and carry no semantic meaning
- Violet opacity cycle: 55–100% over 4s duration, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Teal opacity cycle: 50–100% over 4s duration, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Blur radius: 80px
- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`: static opacity at 75% (middle of cycle) for each glow, no pulsing, 80px blur maintained

## Nav Shell
- Top bar: Health360 wordmark left (text, not avatar), account indicator right (44×44pt minimum)
- Logged-out state: muted ghost chip labeled "Sign in" in top-right nav position
- No bottom navigation — single-surface chat experience
- Prompt bar pinned to bottom of viewport — always bottom-anchored
- Starter chips in horizontal scrollable row between top bar and prompt bar

## Screens

### Screen 1: Chat Home (Logged-Out Default)

**Purpose:** The primary conversational interface. Logged-out users land here and can interact with the AI without signing in. Serves user stories US-3, US-4, US-5.

**States:** default | loading | conversation-active | empty | error | session-expired-error | auth-resolution-failed

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**

**Top Navigation Bar (Fixed, always visible)**
- Background: `--bg` (#0A0A0F)
- Height: 64px on mobile, 56px on web
- Padding: 16px horizontal, 12px vertical
- Layout: flex, space-between

**Left Section (Wordmark only):**
- Health360 wordmark: gradient text (135° violet #7C6FCD → teal #4FAFA8), system-ui 20px 600 weight
- No tagline in top nav

**Right Section (Account Indicator + Sign-In CTA):**

*Mobile Layout (viewport width < 640px):*
- Static text label "Not signed in" hidden on mobile
- Interactive "Sign in" chip only: rounded pill (48px border-radius), teal border 15% opacity (#4FAFA8), transparent background, primary text (#F8F8F7), system-ui 12px 500 weight, padding 16px, min-height 44px, minimum 44×44pt touch target, right-aligned

*Web Layout (viewport width ≥ 640px):*
- Two elements: static text label + interactive sign-in chip
  1. Static text label "Not signed in": 12px system-ui 500 weight, text color rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), always visible, non-dismissible, read-only (no click, no hover state)
  2. Interactive "Sign in" chip: rounded pill (48px border-radius), teal border 15% opacity (#4FAFA8), transparent background, primary text (#F8F8F7), system-ui 12px 500 weight, padding 16px, min-height 44px, minimum 44×44pt touch target, positioned 8px right of static label. On hover (web): border-color increases to teal 30% opacity, background increases to rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.05). On tap: opens Auth Sheet.

- Logged-in state: user's first name (or email if first name unavailable), 12px system-ui 500 weight, text color #F8F8F7, clickable to open account menu with sign-out option

**Conversation Area (Scrollable, centered)**

*Mobile (viewport width < 640px):*
- Full viewport width, padding: 16px horizontal
- Full-height scrollable container positioned below top bar

*Web (viewport width ≥ 640px):*
- Max-width: 680px
- Full-height scrollable container positioned below top bar
- Centered on viewport

**Empty State (Logged-out, no conversation history):**
- Centered "Health360" heading (gradient text, 28px, 600 weight)
- Tagline "All your health. One conversation." directly below (muted text rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), system-ui 12px 400 weight, 4px gap between heading and tagline)

**Conversation-Active State (Logged-out or Logged-in):**
- User and AI message bubbles render sequentially (User Message Bubbles and AI Response Bubbles styling)
- Vertical spacing between messages: 16px gap
- Starter chips fade out on first AI message: 150ms parallel fade-out, ease-out easing (removed from DOM after fade completes or hidden with display: none)
- Prompt bar remains pinned to bottom, always accessible
- Loading indicator (if AI is responding): spinner (12px diameter) + "Thinking..." text appears below last user message

**Loading State (Initial page load, auth resolution in progress):**
- Same layout as Empty State (centered "Health360" heading + tagline)
- No skeleton screens, spinners, or placeholder elements in the conversation area
- Top nav and prompt bar render normally
- Auth resolution occurs silently in the background; UI defaults to logged-out mode immediately

**Session Expiry Warning (Non-Dismissible Banner — Mobile & Web)**

**Purpose:** Non-dismissible warning banner displayed when a logged-out user's session is 10 minutes from expiry AND they have at least one conversation exchange. Serves user stories US-5, US-7.

**Layout:**
- Fixed position at top of conversation area, below the top nav bar
- Full width, 12px padding (horizontal and vertical)
- Height: 48px (min-height to accommodate single-line text and button)
- Background: rgba(229, 192, 123, 0.10) (warning accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: none
- Border-radius: 12px
- Layout: flex, space-between, center-aligned

**Content:**
- Left section: Warning text "Your conversation will expire in 10 minutes. Sign in to preserve it." (warning text color #e5c07b, system-ui 14px 400 weight)
- Right section: "Sign in now" button (rounded pill, 48px border-radius, linear-gradient(135deg, #7C6FCD, #4FAFA8) background, primary text #F8F8F7, system-ui 12px 500 weight, padding 16px, minimum 44×44pt touch target). On tap: opens Auth Sheet (default-returning state).

**Behavior:**
- Non-dismissible — no close affordance, no swipe-to-dismiss, no scrim tap dismiss
- Automatically clears when user performs an action that resets inactivity timer (keystroke, message send, tap) — removed from DOM or hidden with display: none
- Immediately removed from DOM when session expires and Session Expired Error state appears
- If user taps "Sign in now": Auth Sheet opens; if successful, user is taken to Chat Home with conversation preserved

**Session Expired Error State:**
- Inline error banner appears at top of conversation area, above message thread
- Height: 48px (min-height to accommodate single-line text and button)
- Background: rgba(224, 108, 117, 0.10) (error accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: 1px solid #e06c75 (error color)
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px 16px
- Error text: "Your session has expired. Sign in to continue." (#e06c75, system-ui 14px 400 weight)
- "Sign in" button: right-aligned within banner, rounded pill (48px border-radius), #e06c75 border 1px, transparent background, #e06c75 text, system-ui 12px 500 weight, padding 16px, minimum 44×44pt touch target. On tap: opens Auth Sheet.
- Dismiss icon: "X" icon, left side of banner, size 20×20px, touch target 44×44pt, color rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), tappable to dismiss error (user can dismiss and sign in later)
- Error persists until user taps "Sign in" button or dismisses it

**Auth Resolution Failed State:**
- Inline retry affordance appears at top of conversation area, above message thread
- Height: 48px (min-height to accommodate single-line text and button)
- Background: rgba(229, 192, 123, 0.10) (warning accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: 1px solid #e5c07b (warning color)
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px 16px
- Warning text: "Unable to connect. Retrying..." (#e5c07b, system-ui 14px 400 weight)
- "Retry" button: right-aligned within affordance, rounded pill (48px border-radius), #e5c07b border 1px, transparent background, #e5c07b text, system-ui 12px 500 weight, padding 16px, minimum 44×44pt touch target. On tap: attempts authentication state resolution again.
- Dismiss icon: "X" icon, left side of affordance, size 20×20px, touch target 44×44pt, color rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), tappable to dismiss retry prompt (user remains logged out and can retry at any time)
- Affordance persists until user taps "Retry", dismisses it, or navigates away from page

**Starter Chips Row (Positioned between conversation area and prompt bar)**
- Horizontal scrollable row, 16px gap between chips
- Chips displayed as rounded pills (48px border-radius), teal border 15% opacity (#4FAFA8), dark surface background (#13131A)
- Chip labels (default unauthenticated state): "How's my sleep?", "Log a workout", "Check my trends", "Nutrition insights"
- Chips fade out on first AI message: 150ms parallel fade-out, ease-out easing
- Removed from DOM after fade completes (or hidden with display: none)

**Prompt Bar (Pinned to bottom of viewport)**
- Always accessible, never scrolls out of view
- Rounded pill container (48px border-radius), teal border 15% opacity (#4FAFA8), dark surface background (#13131A)
- Input field inside: placeholder text "Ask anything about your health" in subtle text (rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.18))
- Padding: 16px
- Glow effect positioned behind prompt bar: dual radial glows (violet + teal) pulsing asynchronously, 80px blur radius, violet 55–100% opacity cycle, teal 50–100% opacity cycle, 4s duration, offset -1.8s on teal

### Screen 1B: Chat Home (Logged-In Default)

**Purpose:** The authenticated home screen. Users land here after successful sign-in, with their logged-out conversation immediately visible. Serves user stories US-1, US-2, US-8.

**States:** loading | conversation-active | carry-over-failed

**Loading State (Conversation carry-over in progress after authentication completes):**

When a user successfully authenticates and conversation carry-over is in progress:

- Conversation area displays the empty state layout (centered "Health360" heading + tagline "All your health. One conversation.")
- Below the heading, a loading indicator appears: spinner (12px diameter, continuous rotation, 1s per rotation, linear easing, violet color #7C6FCD) + "Loading your conversation..." text (muted text rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), system-ui 12px 400 weight, 8px gap left of spinner)
- Spinner and text centered vertically and horizontally within the conversation area
- Top nav and prompt bar render normally
- Loading state lasts until conversation data is received from the backend (maximum 5 seconds before timing out to error state)
- On completion, loading state is replaced by conversation-active state with all carry-over messages visible

**Carry-Over-Failed State (Logged-in, conversation carry-over unsuccessful):**

When a user successfully authenticates but the backend cannot recover their logged-out conversation:

- Inline explanation message appears at top of conversation area, above message thread
- Background: rgba(229, 192, 123, 0.10) (warning accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: 1px solid #e5c07b (warning color)
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px 16px
- Message text: "You're signed in. Your previous conversation could not be recovered." (#e5c07b, system-ui 14px 400 weight)
- No dismiss affordance, no button — message remains visible at top of conversation area
- User can begin a new conversation immediately below this message
- No retry mechanism offered

**Conversation-Active State (Logged-in, conversation carry-over successful):**

- All messages from logged-out session immediately visible in conversation thread
- System warning messages (session expiry, navigation-away warnings) excluded from thread
- In-conversation nudge excluded from thread
- User draft in prompt bar (if any) preserved across auth transition
- Starter chips are hidden (display: none) whenever the conversation thread contains at least one message

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**

Identical to Screen 1 logged-out default, with the following modifications:

**Top Navigation Bar — Logged-In Variant:**
- Right Section: user's first name (or email if first name unavailable), 12px system-ui 500 weight, text color #F8F8F7, clickable to open account menu with sign-out option
- "Not signed in" static label is hidden
- No "Sign in" chip visible

**Prompt Bar:**
- Identical to logged-out state
- Input is now active and allows message sending (authenticated user)
- Glow effect continues to pulse independently

**All other elements** (spacing, typography, colors, animations) inherit from Screen 1 logged-out default.

### Screen 2: Auth Sheet

**Purpose:** SSO authentication interface. Opened from "Sign in" chip in top nav or warning modal CTAs. Serves user stories US-1, US-2, US-6.

**States:** default | loading | error

**Default State:**

The Auth Sheet displays identical UI and messaging for all users, regardless of account status. The SSO provider handles distinguishing between account creation (new user) and sign-in (returning user) transparently.

- Heading: "Welcome back." (primary text #F8F8F7, system-ui 20px 600 weight, centered)
- Subheading below heading: "Your conversation will be saved when you sign in." (muted text rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), system-ui 14px 400 weight, centered, 12px gap below heading)

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**

**Mobile:**
- Bottom sheet, slides up from bottom over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Full viewport height or content-driven height (whichever fits)
- Rounded top corners: 16px border-radius
- Background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Padding: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical (top and bottom)

**Web:**
- Centered modal overlay
- Fades in + scales up (0.95 → 1.0) over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Max-width: 400px
- Rounded corners: 16px border-radius
- Background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Scrim (darkened background): rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), clickable to dismiss
- Padding: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical

**Header Section:**
- Close button: "X" icon, top-right corner, 44×44pt minimum, transparent background, muted text (#F8F8F7 at 45% opacity) on hover/focus
- Heading and subheading as defined above

**SSO Buttons Section:**
- Two buttons stacked vertically, 16px gap between them
- Each button: rounded pill (48px border-radius), teal border 15% opacity (#4FAFA8), `--surface` background (#13131A), 56px height minimum, full width, padding 16px
- Button text: "Sign in with Google" and "Sign in with Apple" (system-ui 16px 500 weight, primary text #F8F8F7)
- Glow effect: violet-teal dual radial glows pulsing independently behind buttons, violet 55–100% opacity cycle, teal 50–100% opacity cycle, 4s duration, offset -1.8s on teal, 80px blur radius
- Glow positioned absolutely behind button container, not clipped
- On hover (web only): border-color increases to teal 30% opacity, background increases to rgba(19, 19, 26, 1)
- On focus: same styling as hover, outline visible
- On active/press (mobile): background darkens, button remains tappable

**Loading State:**
- Triggered on SSO provider redirect
- Both buttons disable: opacity 0.6, no pointer events
- The tapped button's label changes to "Continuing with [Provider]..."
- Spinner appears left of text inside the tapped button (12px diameter, continuous rotation, 1s per full rotation, linear easing, primary text color)
- Sheet remains open, not dismissible during loading

**Error State:**
- Triggered if SSO provider is unavailable or authentication fails
- Error message displays above buttons: "Sign in failed. Please try again." (error text #e06c75, system-ui 14px 400 weight, centered, 16px margin below subheading)
- Both buttons re-enable and are tappable again
- Close button remains functional — user can dismiss and try again later
- Retry button always remains visible and can be tapped unlimited times; clicking it triggers another authentication attempt

**Dismiss Behavior:**
- Escape key closes sheet
- Scrim tap (web only) closes sheet
- Close button ("X") closes sheet
- On close: sheet slides down (mobile) or fades out + scales down (web) over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Focus returns to trigger element (the "Sign in" chip or CTA button that opened the sheet)
- Sheet does not dismiss during loading state

**Accessibility:**
- Sheet role: dialog, aria-modal="true", aria-labelledby="auth-sheet-heading"
- Heading element: h1 with id="auth-sheet-heading"
- Close button: aria-label="Close.", visible focus state
- Focus trap: Tab cycles through close button → subheading → Google button → Apple button → close button (loop)
- Escape key triggers close
- Screen reader announces: "Dialog: Welcome back. Your conversation will be saved when you sign in."
- SSO buttons read as: "Button: Sign in with Google.", "Button: Sign in with Apple."
- Error message is announced on state change

**Interactions & Animations:**
- Entry: 0.35s slide-up (mobile) or fade-in + scale (web)
- Exit: 0.35s slide-down (mobile) or fade-out + scale (web)
- Button hover: 0.20s border and background transition
- Loading spinner: continuous rotation, respects motion preference
- Glow: independent violet-teal pulse, violet 55–100% opacity, teal 50–100% opacity over 4s, offset -1.8s on teal

**Notes:**
- The glow effect is positioned as a fixed or absolute background layer behind the SSO button container — it extends beyond button edges
- Glow is decorative only (aria-hidden="true")
- No shadow or elevation effect on buttons — only the glow provides depth
- On success (after SSO completes), the sheet dismisses automatically and the user is taken to Chat Home (logged-in state) with conversation preserved
- Session expiry or auth failure during SSO is handled gracefully: user sees error message, buttons re-enable, user can retry or close

### Screen 3: Navigate-Away Warning Modal

**Purpose:** Displayed when a logged-out user attempts to navigate away or close the tab AND they have at least one conversation exchange. Serves user story US-6.

**States:** default

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**

**Mobile:**
- Bottom sheet, slides up from bottom over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Rounded top corners: 16px border-radius
- Background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Padding: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical

**Web:**
- Centered modal overlay
- Fades in + scales up (0.95 → 1.0) over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Max-width: 400px
- Rounded corners: 16px border-radius
- Background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Scrim (darkened background): rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), not clickable to dismiss (modal is non-dismissible)
- Padding: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical

**Header Section:**
- No close button (modal has no dismiss affordance)
- Heading: "Your conversation will be lost." (primary text #F8F8F7, system-ui 16px 600 weight, centered)
- Body copy below heading: "If you leave, your conversation won't be saved unless you sign in first." (muted text rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), system-ui 14px 400 weight, centered, 12px gap below heading)

**CTA Buttons Section:**
- Two buttons stacked vertically, 16px gap between them
- First button (primary): "Sign in." (rounded pill 48px border-radius, gradient background 135° violet #7C6FCD → teal #4FAFA8, primary text #F8F8F7, system-ui 16px 500 weight, 56px height minimum, full width, padding 16px). On tap: opens Auth Sheet (default state); if successful, user is taken to Chat Home with conversation preserved.
- Second button (secondary): "Leave anyway." (rounded pill 48px border-radius, error border 1px solid #e06c75, transparent background, error text #e06c75, system-ui 16px 500 weight, 56px height minimum, full width, padding 16px). On hover: background increases to rgba(224, 108, 117, 0.05), 0.20s ease transition. On tap: permits navigation or tab closure to proceed; conversation is lost.
- On focus: visible outline

**Dismiss Behavior:**
- No Escape key close — user must choose one of two buttons
- No scrim tap close (web) — user must choose one of two buttons
- No close button affordance
- User must explicitly choose "Sign in." or "Leave anyway." to proceed

**Accessibility:**
- Modal role: dialog, aria-modal="true", aria-labelledby="navigate-away-heading", aria-describedby="navigate-away-description"
- Heading element: h2 with id="navigate-away-heading"
- Body copy element: p with id="navigate-away-description"
- No close button — focus trap: Tab cycles through Sign in button → Leave anyway button → Sign in button (loop)
- Escape key does NOT close modal
- Screen reader announces: "Dialog: Your conversation will be lost. If you leave, your conversation won't be saved unless you sign in first."
- Buttons read as: "Button: Sign in.", "Button: Leave anyway."

**Interactions & Animations:**
- Entry: 0.35s slide-up (mobile) or fade-in + scale (web)
- Exit: 0.35s slide-down (mobile) or fade-out + scale (web) — triggered only after user chooses one of two buttons
- Button hover: 0.20s background transition

**Notes:**
- This modal is non-dismissible — the user must choose one of two explicit actions to proceed.
- Clicking "Leave anyway." allows the browser navigation or tab closure to proceed; the conversation is immediately lost.
- Clicking "Sign in." opens the Auth Sheet; on successful authentication, the user is taken to Chat Home and the navigation attempt is prevented (the modal closes and user remains in chat).
- Browser-level force closes (e.g., force-closing the app on mobile) cannot be intercepted by this modal.

### Screen 4: In-Conversation Sign-In Nudge

**Purpose:** A conversational system message displayed after the first complete AI response in a logged-out session. Informs the user that their conversation will not be saved unless they sign in. Rendered as part of the message thread. Serves user stories US-3, US-7.

**States:** default

**Trigger:**
- Fires exactly once per logged-out session, after the first complete AI response is delivered to the user
- A new logged-out session begins each time the user is in an unauthenticated state; if the user signs in and then signs out again within the same browser window, that constitutes a new logged-out session and the nudge fires again (on the next first AI response)
- Not displayed to authenticated users

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**

**Nudge Container:**
- Rendered as a system message within the conversation thread (left-aligned, same positioning as AI response bubbles)
- Background: rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.10) (violet accent surface, 10% opacity)
- Border: 1px solid rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.18) (violet accent border)
- Border-radius: 12px
- Padding: 12px 16px
- Margin: 12px left, 12px right
- Max-width: 85% of conversation width (mobile) or 600px (web)
- Entry animation: opacity 0 to 1 over 150ms ease-out, no transform

**Content:**
- Heading: "Your conversation won't be saved." (violet accent text #7C6FCD, system-ui 14px 600 weight)
- Body text below heading: "Sign in or sign up to keep your chat history for later." (muted text rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), system-ui 12px 400 weight, 8px gap below heading)
- Two buttons:
  1. "Sign in." button: rounded pill (48px border-radius), linear-gradient(135deg, #7C6FCD, #4FAFA8) background, primary text #F8F8F7, system-ui 12px 500 weight, padding 16px, minimum 44×44pt touch target. On tap: opens Auth Sheet (default state).
  2. "Sign up." button: rounded pill (48px border-radius), violet border 1px solid rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.18), transparent background, violet text #7C6FCD, system-ui 12px 500 weight, padding 16px, minimum 44×44pt touch target. On tap: opens Auth Sheet (default state).
- Button layout: stacked vertically on mobile (viewport width < 480px); side-by-side horizontally on web (viewport width ≥ 480px)
- Gap between buttons: 8px

**Accessibility:**
- Nudge role: region, aria-label="Sign in reminder."
- Heading element: h3 (semantic heading for the nudge)
- Body text is plain text, not a label
- Both buttons are aria-labeled and have visible focus states
- Screen reader announces: "Region: Sign in reminder. Your conversation won't be saved. Sign in or sign up to keep your chat history for later. Button: Sign in. Button: Sign up."

**Interactions & Animations:**
- Nudge appears with fade-in animation: opacity 0 to 1 over 150ms ease-out
- Buttons have standard hover state (0.20s transition on border/background)
- Nudge cannot be dismissed — it remains in the conversation thread permanently

**Notes:**
- The nudge is excluded from conversation carry-over when the user signs in — it does not appear in the user's conversation history on the authenticated home screen (AC#13)
- The nudge appears only once per logged-out session; if the user dismisses it (by ignoring it and continuing to chat), it does not re-trigger
- The nudge is rendered as a system message, not as a modal or overlay, so it does not disrupt the conversation flow
- On successful sign-in from either button, the Auth Sheet closes and the user is taken to Chat Home with their logged-out conversation preserved (minus the nudge itself)
- Both "Sign in." and "Sign up." buttons open the Auth Sheet in the same default state — the SSO provider transparently handles distinguishing between account creation and sign-in

## User Flows

### Flow: US-1 — New user sign-up
Chat Home (logged-out, empty) → [taps "Sign in" chip or in-conversation nudge CTA] → Auth Sheet (default) → [completes Google or Apple SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, loading) → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active, logged-out conversation immediately visible)

### Flow: US-2 — Returning user sign-in
Chat Home (logged-out, default) → [taps "Sign in" chip] → Auth Sheet (default) → [completes Google or Apple SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, loading) → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active, logged-out conversation immediately visible)

### Flow: US-3 — Explore before sign-up
Chat Home (logged-out, default) → [interacts with chat, sends first message] → Chat Home (conversation-active) → [receives first AI response] → In-Conversation Sign-In Nudge displays → [continues chatting without signing in]

### Flow: US-4 — Persistent logged-out indicator
Chat Home (logged-out) — "Not signed in" static text label persistently visible in top-right (web only) throughout all logged-out states. Static text only, no modal, popover, tooltip, or CTA element. Ambient awareness of authentication status only.

### Flow: US-5 — Session expiry warning and conversation recovery
Chat Home (conversation-active, logged-out) → [10 minutes remaining on session TTL] → Session Expiry Warning banner displays → [user taps "Sign in now"] → Auth Sheet (default, loading) → [completes SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active, logged-out conversation immediately visible)

Alt: user ignores banner → continues chatting → session expires → conversation lost, Session Expired Error displays.

### Flow: US-6 — Navigate-away protection
Chat Home (conversation-active, logged-out) → [user attempts back, close tab, or navigate away] → Navigate-Away Warning Modal displays → [user taps "Sign in"] → Auth Sheet (default) → [completes SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active, logged-out conversation immediately visible)

Alt: user taps "Leave anyway" → navigation proceeds, conversation lost.

### Flow: US-7 — Conversation preserved on sign-up from logged-out session
Auth Sheet (opened from any CTA, default state) → [completes SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation-active) → logged-out conversation history immediately visible in conversation thread (system messages and nudge excluded)

## Accessibility

**Color & Contrast**
- Text primary (#F8F8F7) on background (#0A0A0F): contrast ratio 14.8:1 (WCAG AAA).
- Text primary on surface (#13131A): contrast ratio 13.6:1 (WCAG AAA).
- CTA text (white) on accent gradient: maintains WCAG AA minimum across full gradient range.
- Error text (#e06c75) on background: contrast ratio 4.8:1 (WCAG AA).
- Glow treatment: decorative only, carries no meaning. Screen readers unaffected.
- Color is never the only signal for state change. Every state change accompanied by label, icon, or structural change.

**Interactive Elements**
- Logged-out indicator: non-interactive text, informational only.
- Logged-in account name: clickable text with 44×44pt minimum touch target. Screen reader label: "Account menu trigger. [User name]. Activate to open account menu."
- Sign in CTAs: 44×44pt minimum touch targets across all screens.
- Session expiry and navigate-away modals: both CTA buttons 44×44pt minimum.
- In-conversation nudge: "Sign in" button 44×44pt minimum.
- All buttons have visible focus states (outline or background highlight).

**Modals & Sheets**
- Auth Sheet, Session Expiry Warning, and Navigate-Away modal all trap focus while open.
- Escape key closes Auth Sheet and Session Expiry Warning (dismissible).
- Escape key does NOT close Navigate-Away modal. User must choose one of two buttons to proceed.
- Focus returned to trigger element after sheet/modal closes.

**Keyboard Navigation (Web)**
- All interactive elements reachable by Tab.
- Tab order: top-left wordmark (non-interactive) → top-right account indicator (clickable) → prompt bar input → Auth Sheet (if open, cycles through close → buttons → close).
- Modal/sheet focus loops prevent accidental escape.
- No keyboard traps.

**Screen Readers**
- Page title reads: "Health360 — Chat with your AI health coach."
- Wordmark: ARIA role presentation (duplicates page title).
- Tagline reads as static text.
- In-conversation nudge: Screen reader reads "Region: Sign-in reminder. Your conversation won't be saved. Sign in or sign up to keep your chat history for later. Button: Sign in."
- Session expiry warning: "Dialog: Your conversation will expire in 10 minutes. Sign in to preserve it."
- Navigate-away warning: "Dialog: Your conversation will be lost. If you leave, your conversation won't be saved unless you sign in first."
- All CTAs include full context before button label.

**RTL Support**
- Layout mirrors for RTL languages (Arabic, Hebrew).
- Logo moves to right, account indicator to left.
- Wordmark and tagline remain centered.
- Starter chips scroll direction reverses.
- Accent gradient direction reverses (135° becomes 315°).
- All text alignment and spacing mirrors appropriately.
- In-conversation nudge: "Sign in" button aligns left (mobile) or right (web) in RTL.

**Mobile & Touch**
- One-thumb reachability: all primary actions in bottom third of screen on mobile.
- Bottom sheets for all modals on mobile (native interaction model).
- Minimum 44×44pt touch targets, no exceptions.

**Motion**
- Glow animation respects prefers-reduced-motion: if enabled, glow opacity is static at 75% (middle of cycle) with no pulsing, 80px blur maintained.
- All transitions respect user motion preferences.
- Loading spinners pause when prefers-reduced-motion is enabled.

## Design System Updates

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO DESIGN_SYSTEM.md — Interactive Element Styling]

**Rounded Pill Containers (Prompt Bar, Auth Buttons, CTA Buttons, Sign-In Chip)**

```css
border-radius: 48px;
border: 1px solid rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.15);   /* teal at 15% opacity */
background: rgba(19, 19, 26, 0.8);
padding: 16px;
transition: all 0.20s ease;

&:hover {
  border-color: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.30);
  background: rgba(19, 19, 26, 1);
}

&:focus-within {
  border-color: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.30);
  background: rgba(19, 19, 26, 1);
}
```

**Primary CTA (Gradient Background)**

```css
background: linear-gradient(135deg, #7C6FCD, #4FAFA8);
border: none;
color: #F8F8F7;
border-radius: 48px;
padding: 16px;
transition: opacity 0.20s ease;

&:hover {
  opacity: 0.9;
}

&:active {
  opacity: 0.85;
}
```

**Secondary CTA (Teal Outline)**

```css
border: 1px solid rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.15);
background: transparent;
color: #F8F8F7;
border-radius: 48px;
padding: 16px;
transition: all 0.20s ease;

&:hover {
  border-color: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.30);
  background: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.05);
}

&:focus {
  border-color: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.30);
  outline: 2px solid rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.5);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

**Destructive CTA (Error Outline)**

```css
border: 1px solid #e06c75;
background: transparent;
color: #e06c75;
border-radius: 48px;
padding: 16px;
transition: all 0.20s ease;

&:hover {
  background: rgba(224, 108, 117, 0.05);
}

&:focus {
  border-color: #e06c75;
  outline: 2px solid rgba(224, 108, 117, 0.5);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

Glow effect positioned behind: radial halo, violet 55–100% opacity, teal 50–100% opacity, 80px blur, independent animation per instance (offset -1.8s on teal).

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

## Open Questions

(none)

## Design Assumptions

Designed for max 60-minute unauthenticated session TTL with inactivity-based expiry and a 10-minute warning threshold — conversation carry-over assumes backend can recover logged-out session data for up to 60 minutes post-expiry.
onboarding.productSpec:
# Onboarding — Product Spec

## Problem
New and returning users need a frictionless path from first landing to the main chat home screen. Unauthenticated users risk losing their conversation history without realizing it, which undermines trust and conversion. The onboarding experience must get users to the home screen quickly, make the value of signing in clear without forcing it, and ensure no one is surprised by data loss.

## Target Users
- **New users** — arriving for the first time, no existing account
- **Returning users** — have an existing account, signing back in
- **Logged-out new users** — new users who choose to explore before signing up

## User Stories
1. As a new user, I want to sign up quickly so that I can start using Health360 without unnecessary friction.
2. As a returning user, I want to sign in and land directly on the home screen so that I can pick up where I left off.
3. As a logged-out user, I want to explore the product before committing to sign up so that I can understand its value first.
4. As a logged-out user, I want a subtle, persistent indicator that I am not signed in so that I am never surprised by the implications of being logged out.
5. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned before my session expires so that I can sign up and save my conversation before it's lost.
6. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned when I try to navigate away or close the tab so that I don't accidentally lose my conversation.
7. As a logged-out user, I want to know that signing in will preserve my conversation so that I have a clear reason to create an account at that moment.
8. As a new user completing sign-up from a logged-out session, I want my existing conversation to carry over so that I don't lose the context I've already built.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Unauthenticated users can access the chat interface and interact with the AI without signing in.
2. A persistent, non-dismissible indicator is visible to logged-out users displaying the text "Not signed in".
3. The indicator is read-only, displays no interactive elements, does not overlay other UI, and does not trigger any prompts or modals on its own.
4. The logged-out indicator remains visible at all times while the user is not authenticated, and disappears immediately upon valid authentication token receipt, before conversation history loads.
5. New users can sign up via SSO (Google, Apple Sign-In) only — no password-based registration.
6. Returning users can sign in via SSO and are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
7. New users who complete sign-up are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
8. When a logged-out user's session is approaching expiry with 10 minutes of inactivity time remaining AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a non-dismissible warning banner appears at the top of the chat interface with a "Sign in now" call-to-action button; the warning is automatically cleared only when the user performs an action that resets the inactivity timer (e.g., sends a message); the banner cannot be manually dismissed or swiped away.
9. When a logged-out user attempts to navigate away or close the tab AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a modal appears with a primary "Sign in" button and a secondary "Leave anyway" button; the modal has no dismiss affordance and the user must choose one of the two buttons to proceed; clicking "Leave anyway" permits the user to proceed with navigation or tab closure; browser-level force closes cannot be intercepted.
10. After the first complete AI response in a session, an in-conversation nudge surfaces during logged-out sessions only, informing the user that their conversation will not be saved unless they sign in, rendered as a conversational system message with a clear sign-up or sign-in call-to-action; the nudge is not displayed to authenticated users.
11. The in-conversation nudge fires exactly once per logged-out session; a new logged-out session begins each time the user is in an unauthenticated state, and if the user signs in and then signs out again within the same browser window, that constitutes a new logged-out session and the nudge fires again.
12. Both warnings and the in-conversation nudge include a clear call-to-action to sign up or sign in.
13. Upon completing sign-up or sign-in from any warning CTA, the user is taken directly to the main chat home screen and their previous logged-out conversation is immediately visible in the message thread without requiring additional navigation or explicit retrieval; system warning messages and the in-conversation nudge are excluded from the carried-over conversation thread; if conversation carry-over fails, an inline message appears confirming successful sign-in and explaining that the previous conversation could not be recovered.
14. If the logged-out user has no conversation history, no session expiry or navigation warning is shown.
15. When a logged-out user's session expires after inactivity, the chat interface remains interactive but becomes unresponsive — attempts to send a message trigger an inline error message at the top of the chat with a "Sign in" call-to-action; the user can dismiss the error and sign in at any time via the Auth Sheet.
16. When the AI is unavailable during a logged-out exploration session, an inline error message appears at the top of the chat interface; the user can send any new message to trigger a retry attempt; if the AI remains unavailable after a retry, the same inline error reappears and the user can attempt again with another message; the chat interface remains interactive.
17. There is no forced sign-up wall at any point in the logged-out experience.
18. The path from landing on the logged-out chat interface to authenticated home screen requires a maximum of 3 user interactions: (1) landing on the logged-out chat interface, (2) tapping a sign-in or sign-up call-to-action, (3) completing SSO and being taken directly to the authenticated home screen. No intermediate profile setup, goal-setting, or confirmation screens are permitted.
19. Goal setting does not occur during onboarding — it happens post-onboarding through the AI conversation.
20. On initial page load, the UI defaults to logged-out mode immediately while authentication state resolution occurs in the background.
21. If authentication state resolution succeeds during initial load, the logged-out indicator disappears immediately upon valid token receipt and conversation history becomes visible after valid token receipt; any conversation the user started during the auth resolution window is treated as part of the logged-out session and is carried over to the authenticated home screen's message thread; user drafts in the prompt bar are preserved across the auth state transition.
22. If authentication state resolution fails at initial load, the chat remains fully interactive in logged-out mode and a dismissible retry affordance appears inline at the top of the chat interface; the retry affordance persists until the user dismisses it, attempts retry, or navigates away from the page; the user can attempt retry at any point by tapping the affordance again; there is no limit on the number of retry attempts.
23. Unauthenticated sessions expire after 60 minutes of inactivity; each user action (message send, keystroke, tap) resets the inactivity timer.
24. Users land directly on the logged-out chat interface on first visit — there is no separate landing, splash, or onboarding screen; the chat interface is the primary entry point.
25. Tapping any "Sign in" or "Sign in now" call-to-action opens an Auth Sheet overlay displaying SSO provider buttons (Google, Apple Sign-In); the Auth Sheet is dismissible and returns the user to the logged-out chat.
26. Returning users and new users encounter the same Auth Sheet UI with identical SSO provider buttons and messaging; the SSO provider handles distinguishing between account creation (new user) and sign-in (returning user) transparently.
27. If a user's authentication token expires mid-session, the "Not signed in" indicator reappears automatically and all chat functionality becomes read-only — the user can view conversation history but cannot send new messages; attempting to send a message triggers an inline error; the user can sign in again via the Auth Sheet without losing conversation history.
28. Logged-out users receive generic, non-personalized conversational responses from the AI with no goals, no profile context, and no prior session memory; personalized responses and memory-based coaching are authenticated-only features that unlock upon sign-in.

## Edge Cases

- User signs up from the warning CTA but the session expires before authentication completes — conversation cannot be recovered; the user lands on the authenticated home screen with an inline message confirming successful sign-in and explaining that the previous conversation could not be recovered.
- User begins sign-up flow from the warning but abandons it — session continues to expire; if the user returns to the chat before expiry, the warning banner reappears if still within the expiry window.
- Conversation carry-over fails after successful authentication — user lands on the authenticated home screen with an inline message at the top confirming successful sign-in and explaining that the previous conversation could not be recovered; no retry mechanism is offered.
- User's logged-out session expires while they are away from the chat — the next time they attempt to send a message, they receive an inline error and can dismiss it and sign in at any time.
- User's authentication token expires mid-session while they are actively viewing the conversation — the "Not signed in" indicator reappears, chat becomes read-only, and the next message send attempt triggers an inline error; the user can sign in again via Auth Sheet and resume without data loss.

## Non-Goals
- Goal setting, profile building, and AI coaching — these begin after the user reaches the home screen.
- Password-based authentication — SSO only.
- Forced sign-up walls or conversion prompts outside of the defined warning triggers and in-conversation nudge.
- Auto-saving or server-side backup of logged-out conversations without explicit user action.
- Changing session expiry duration — infrastructure concern outside this spec.
- Social or referral flows — not part of the initial vision.

## Open Questions
(none)
